Blight.description: A fungal disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum. Forms large, cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions on leaves.
solution: Apply fungicides containing azoxystrobin or propiconazole at early infection stage.
Remove and destroy heavily infected plant debris.
Apply mancozeb (2g/L) or chlorothalonil spray every 7-10 days.
Ensure proper field drainage to reduce humidity.
prevention: Use certified blight-resistant maize seed varieties.
Practice crop rotation — avoid planting maize in the same field consecutively.
Maintain proper plant spacing for good air circulation.
Avoid overhead irrigation; use drip irrigation instead.
Apply balanced fertilizers — avoid excess nitrogen.

Common_Rust.description: A fungal disease forming small, circular to elongated, golden-brown to brick-red pustules on both leaf surfaces.
solution: Apply triazole-based fungicides (tebuconazole or propiconazole) at first sign of pustules.
Spray mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/L water every 10 days.
Remove severely infected leaves to reduce spore spread.
Apply sulfur-based fungicide as an early intervention.
prevention: Plant rust-resistant hybrid maize varieties.
Early planting to avoid peak rust season.
Monitor crops regularly from the seedling stage.
Avoid excessive use of nitrogen fertilizers.
Ensure good field drainage and air circulation.

Gray_Leaf_Spot.description: A fungal disease producing rectangular, gray to tan lesions with parallel edges, limited by leaf veins.
solution: Apply strobilurin fungicides (azoxystrobin, pyraclostrobin) at tasseling stage.
Spray chlorothalonil or mancozeb at 7-14 day intervals.
Reduce crop residue by deep plowing after harvest.
Apply foliar fungicide when 5% of leaf area shows symptoms.
prevention: Use gray leaf spot resistant or tolerant hybrid varieties.
Practice minimum 2-year crop rotation with non-host crops.
Avoid conservation tillage in high-risk areas.
Plant early to escape high humidity periods.
Ensure proper plant density to reduce canopy moisture.

Healthy.description: The plant shows no signs of disease. Leaves are green, firm and free from spots, rust or blight.
prevention: Continue using certified disease-free seeds.
Maintain balanced fertilization schedule.
Keep proper irrigation and drainage.
Regular field scouting for early disease detection.
Keep surrounding areas free from weed hosts.
